reviews.review_text: If you are looking for great Bush Camping Caravan with excellent facilities this is the place to come. Within easy drive to Jindabyne and the snow fields the park is perfect. Excellent amenities and Camp Kitchen has  everything including a wood heater

Kosciuszko Tourist Park is great! I stayed in the Cedar Cabin and everything was impeccably clean, the location is great. It was well equipped. The dormitory was spacious and the bed very comfortable. The bathroom was quite clean, with great water pressure in the showers. The staff were very friendly and helpful, always willing to assist. I was very satisfied with my stay there.

Loved our visit, lovely campsite with great amenities and plenty of space per site. Great location to explore the national park. Sushil and the staff were very responsive to messages and questions, and upgraded us for free to a nicer chalet! Thank you so much! We’ll definitely be back.
We got in late and S was so good and accommodating. The room was clean, and had everything we needed. Strongly recommend staying here. So convenient!

This was our second time staying 4 nights in a Cedar Cabin, and we always have the best time. The park is quiet this time of year, so there was never an issue using amenities. The cabins are perfect for a family, and then some. We spent all of our time cooking outside by the fire, but the cabin has a gas stove and can facilitate indoor cooking too. The location is very close to Charlotte Pass, and all the walks along there - and close to Jindabyne. There are plenty of walks that you can take from the campsite too. We can’t wait for the next time we stay!
Stayed in one of the older cabins. Very basic, however it was neat, clean, tidy, functional, hot shower etc…and a nice view from the window. Perfect base camp to head out on adventures from.
We are so lucky to able to book this beautiful camping ground to stay 3 night. We were had great time. Thank you.🙏

We spent just over a week camping at Kosciuszko Tourist Park and it was an amazing experience. The park is located in the National Park, so it’s beautiful and leafy with lots of birds and wildlife. We woke up to the warble of Magpies each morning and enjoyed watching the kangaroos. There were beautiful brush tail possums that visited at night. The amenities are spotlessly clean, which included a camp kitchen with fireplace and heated modern amenities blocks with great shower pressure. Amenities blocks are secured with a PIN code for safety. Rakesh and his family were so kind and helpful. You can have a fire right at your campsite, firewood available onsite. Only 15 mins into Jindabyne and 20 mins to Perisher, 25 mins to Charlotte pass for excellent hiking. 30 mins to Thredbo. Excellent spot to base yourself for a alpine adventure. We absolutely loved our stay and will be back to visit again in the future 🌿
Highly recommend, such a great stay and kind people!! Helped me after hours at last minute notice!!
We had an amazing stay in a beautiful setting, right in the middle of nature and surrounded by animals !

Stayed for three night in the Cedar Cabin. The manager Rakesh is awesome such a great guy very helpful. The park is amazing, rooms clean. Toilets and showers all very clean. Cedar Cabin has aircon, two rooms and kitchen with gas cookers even dish washing soap and tea towels. It was way better than many hotels I have been in. We had possums visit the front of our cabin and saw various animals. Such a beautiful place. If I could give 10 stars for cleanliness and service I would. Perfect spot for base camp to access Mt Kocziosko

Always have the best time here. This was my second visit. Previously booked unpowered camping site and this time we booked a cabin. The amenities, the level of cleanliness, helpful and cooperative staff is why I will always choose this place. Rakesh was amazing and very responsive regarding our requests. Super impressed of how this place is kept and gives you a comfortable and homely feeling with camping experience. The best part is you’d always find kangaroos and sometimes deer in the park. Kitchen has everything you’d ever need.

We had a look around Threadbow & Jindabyne area for camps or caravan parks with privacy as we are camping. Not much available, until we found this little cracker. With great hospitality &  beautifully appointed amenities. Lovely bush setting. So happy we booked another night. Cheers Simon K.

Conveniently located to access Thredbo and Perisher valley. Wifi available with super clean campsites and bathrooms. Great location for stargazers. The manager Rakesh is a super friendly and helpful guy. Highly recommend!

We really enjoyed our stay at Kosciusko tourist park, the cabin we were upgraded to was beautiful and newly renovated. Wish we had of had the chance to stay longer. But will definitely be back with the family. Great stay !
Exceptional cabin! Interior is all new and clean. Will definitely come back and recommend to everyone.

Great location. Right after the visitor center and ideal place to stay to hike around the national park! The manager/caretakers were very nice and accomodating! We stayed at the newly renovated chalets and it was perfect!

I stayed here with my partner for my birthday in their premium cabin. The stay was very beautiful. The cabins are nice, clean and very well equipped. It was amazing to see kangaroos roaming around freely. Rakesh was very accommodating and kind. We would definitely come back!

Tried the cabins here 2 years ago, and just recently tried unpowered camping site. Good amenities for campers, bathrooms were really clean and well-provided with tissue paper and hot water (timed). Cabins were also good, some areas needed maintenance but overall had 2 good stays here. Will definitely be back because of the perfect location to ski parks during winter and hiking tracks during summer. Beware of possums though but generally safe when we were there. There’s wifi available for purchase (cheaper if bought online during booking), but Telstra and Optus have mobile coverage (but not super strong).

Booked 4 nights camping - extended it by another 3 because we loved being here. Lots of bushy camp spots - great camp kitchen, and amenities. Everything was really clean. Friendly service - nothing was too much of a bother. Plenty of kangaroos and overly friendly possums - don't leave any food out!! Even got to have a camp fire on 2 nights, which was great. Too warm on the other nights. Would definitely stay again.
1st time we stayed here, we stayed on an unpowered site and we can't wait to come back. Convenient and superclean facilities.

stayed here over christmas for three nights, beautiful views right from the cabin’s windows, super cosy room with everything you need
great bushwalking tracks around the park, walked upon one and it was beautiful!
definitely recommend this tourist park, it’s lovely
Amazing place, best manager in the whole wide world. Great showers and clean toilets. Nice staff and the kindnest of them is unbeatable. Grounds very well kept and maintained.

A nice place for caravaning as it has all the things u need to power and resupply the water tank. Accidently booked for the the wrong one which didnt have water resupply and only electricity and the guy in the office offered to help move us to a closer spot which had a nearby water refill. The guy that works here has great customer service. And the bathrooms are clean

January 4
The manager went out his way to help us. Impressed with the great customer service.

Great park but it has one major problem. The camp kitchen only has one sink. I saw it being used for about 4 hours in a row. The camp kitchen is likewise grim and needs a good reno. Install some more sinks! The shower rooms are good. WiFi also cheap and fast

Went there without a booking. Thought  it was free camping but ended up not being free camping, Manger came up to politely told us that it wasn't offered us the site and gave us discount for the trouble. Only other problem is people riding dirt bikes around loudly at 9pm
Camp-site was clean and clear with great amenities very close by! Great location as well!

Kosciuszko Tourist Park 에 7박 8일 stay 하면서 좋은 추억 만들었네요^^

Thredbo 까진 40분, Jindabyne 10분, Charlotte pass lookout 25분 그리고 Yarrangobilly caves 까진 차로 2시간 걸립니다.

Kosciuszko 산 정상 도전으로 다양한 루트로 도전하고 싶다면 여기를 추천 드리고 등산 후 바베큐도 맛나게 해 먹었어요 ㅎㅎ

관리하는 staff 이 친절하고 신경 잘 써줍니다.
모두들 안전하게 즐거운 여행 하세요^^

Thanks Boss. We're No.13 couple . Hope you remember us^^
Clean and good facilities 👍

Great time here and good location. (Charlotte pass lookout 25 mins, 10 mins Jindabyne, Thredbo 40 mins by car)

Staff are very kind and helpful.
Price is reasonable and all facilities are well maintained.

This was our second stay at the tourist park, and once again it was a wonderful experience. We stayed in Cabin 9 overlooking the bush, where wallabies and kangaroos were a daily sight, and even some possum friends joined us around the outdoor fireplace 😀.

The park management was extremely friendly and helpful. The self-contained cabin had everything four of us needed, and considering the unique location within the national park, the accommodation offered excellent value for money.

We will definitely be booking our next stay here.

Couldn’t fault this place. The location is epic, just 20 minutes from Charlotte’s Pass. The facilities were excellent all very clean and well maintained.

The staff were very helpful and accommodating.

I would definitely recommend this place to anyone looking for a stay in the national park
This must be one of the most underrated caravan parks in the Snowies. Great location, camping sites, cabins, amenities and common room. The staff are very friendly and helpful. Highly recommended.

Fantastic cabin with newly renovated bathroom and kitchen. Loved the in floor heating in bathroom. New bedding. Quiet bushland setting teaming with wildlife. Excellent location for mountains and Jindabyne

My son and I stayed here twice during our trip to the mountains… both times when we were washed out at our campsite!!! The cabins were comfortable and cozy and the park is very peaceful . The staff were really helpful and friendly. We were also very surprised how good the facilities were!! The shower and toilets were clean and modern. We’d definitely come back again 👍
We stayed as a self contained caravan but had power, water and access to the facilities. Everything was pristine and clean with a beautiful bush setting. We would definitely recommend staying here!

Great location, 10 min to Jindabyne.
Quiet park. Basic rooms but thats all you need.
Rooms are clean.
Loved the wildlife in the park.
Staff/park manager not often around.
Dont attempt to check in early there will be no one there.
Great location for weekend escape. Plenty of trekking trails nearby. Helpful management and really nice cottage.

We stayed in the 2 bedroom chalet upgrade. It was very comfortable and the bathroom was excellent. Really loved the heated floor. The kitchen was modern and had all the essentials. The park location is wonderful and we saw lots of kangaroos, deer and possums. We had a wonderful stay and will definitely visit again.
The guys here have been great. Had to change dates 3 times because of illness and they did all they could to help us.

Camping here for 1 week. Location great for bush walks. Naive of having to also pay National park fees just to get to camp ground. Amenities immaculate and well set up. Not sure  how they cope if the park was full. Yes the possums are extremely friendly!

This park is spacious and very well equipped in a true bush setting. The new management is very friendly and extremely helpful. We had a few requests over the weekend and they were all dealt with very promptly and politely.
The bathrooms are recently renovated and in excellent condition. The camp kitchen is very large with plenty of appliances, although we only used it to wash-up.
We stayed three nights with a camper trailer on a powered site. Fireplaces are great and access is pretty good for parking trailers. A neighbour said their cabin was very cosy and nice to stay in.
Location is excellent - very convenient to Jindabyne and easy to access trails from Guthega and Thredbo River picnic area
The possums are super friendly so lock-up your food!
Affordable, close to Perisher, beautiful bush walks, nice facilities and very responsive, service oriented owner and staff. Recommended!
Great place and service, also kids loved ittt!!!

We were previously recurrent customers. We contacted them to cancel a trip due to a family emergency as our dog had a spinal cord injury 2 days prior to the trip requiring surgery and needed to be cared for and they did not provide any refund or even credit for a future booking or allow us to reschedule despite us being loyal customers and having a serious unforeseeable family emergency. Then the following trip which we had paid a deposit for they sent us a notification to pay the remaining balance 9 days prior to the trip, but we were at a wedding, and the following day was fathers day so we only got around to paying the outstanding balance 7 days before the trip. Then when we went to pay we saw they had cancelled our booking and they kept our original deposit.

We camped at Kosi Tourist Park with our Caravan. We found that the amenities were clean, the location was great as it was close to Mt Perisher, good value for money and quiet camp. The only nuisance were the possums that are not afraid of people and will not let you eat outside, otherwise they will take the food. So better to eat indoors at night time.
Have camped here many times in powered and unpowered sites. Lovely bushy surrounds, modern clean bathroom block, and very family friendly.

I strongly advise avoiding this campsite. Without any prior notice, they charged my card well before the agreed payment date. My booking was then cancelled at the last minute, and I was told I needed to pay more than originally agreed, despite our arrangement clearly stating that the second half of the payment would be due on the day of arrival. It appears they operate this way to secure last-minute bookings at higher rates, leaving existing customers stranded.

When I called to address the issue, the staff were not only unhelpful but openly rude and dismissive. This level of unprofessionalism is unacceptable, especially in the hospitality industry where customer service should be a priority. Save yourself the stress—there are many other campsites that honour their agreements and treat their guests with basic respect and courtesy.

Sick park nice and close to Perisher. Have stayed here previously and had a decent time but the current management went out of their way to get my son and I a cabin last minute on a Saturday afternoon and got the cabin ready within the hour. Legends, definitely prefer this park to the ones in town when I’m here for the snow.

It's expensive which is understandable, but the service lets it down. The heater in the hut ran all night and in the morning it was still below 15c.
There was a scheduled power outage however we were never informed of it until the power went off mid way through cooking breakfast on electric cooker. When attended the service desk there was a note letting us know about the scheduled maintenance. Checked in a day earlier and nothing mentioned.

Nice location and close to perisher.
Only things it needs are newer fire places at most of the unpowered sites.

Absolutely fun time had beautiful scenery wildlife kept us all amused from deers to possums , trail walks great with so many to choose from ,close to jinderbyne and perisher , the cabin was lovely clean and very homely ,staff went over and above for us nothing to much trouble for them and staff on site very friendly a big thankyou from our family for making it great holiday.

Awesome place to stay highly recommended, staff are fantastic houseing keeping do a fantastic job they go out of there way for u. all staff are friendly i would come stay here again a big thank u from our family for makeing our stay a perfect and memorable one 😀
Genuinely helpful staff, comfortable hut. Great stay.

For $70/night for a patch of dirt I was expecting a lot more than what we got. I honestly can't believe that you can charge this much money to camp in my own tent and then time the showers with a broken system that turns yours off for 2 mins when someone else's time runs out, charge extra for wifi, and charge extra for the laundry. This place used to be great before new owners ruined it. What did the $70/night get me if not showers, laundry and wifi? Literally a piece of dirt. There used to be a free campsite next to this place but they had the government remove it. Horribly overpriced.
Very welcoming and friendly staff, very accommodating and understanding. Very highly recommend to stay here.

Nice and close

They do well to maintain the park

Amazing service, very helpful when needed and loved the included fireplace. Amazing location so close to perisher and Jindabyne; perfect for a few nights. Clean and maintained facilities: bathroom, hot showers, kitchen, laundry. Would definitely recommend
Handy place to stop and short drive to perisher

Awesome service from the friendly manager during my stay camping in a tent.
Clean facilities and awesome camp kichen worth every dollar. Drying room, hot showers and clean toilets make camping in the snow very comfortable. Can't fault anything, Thanks again I'll be back
Awesome lake view and a very close destination to  perisher valley

Had a great stay at the hotel! The rooms were clean and comfortable, and the service was excellent. A special thanks to the manager, Rakesh, who was extremely helpful and made our stay even better. Highly recommended!
Amazing facility and hospitality thank you Rakesh for helping us he was best at his job most recommended

Great place to stay, have visited many times.
Highly recommend. Though if you intend to change your booking to include extra people for less than your full stay the booking system has limitations as to what it can do for you.

It was a beautiful place and was a lovely stay. We were so confused because of the early check out but the Manager helps us very well so we enjoyed alot campfire and bbq there good place to stay for going to snowy mountain.
Had a decent stay at this park. Cabin was brand new furnished, service was good

To all the guys go and try to say hi the nicest guy I ever met Rakesh the great very friendly compassionate and take his job very serious been pleasure to meet you and the park was great place to live and very cheap and affordable o would recommend all of you give it good go thank you

We accidentally  booked spot for caravan.
Which was really  good and cheap but we didnt have caravan 😂 so in the middle of thr night we were not expecting any office staff  but we were responded by the manager there supported us to change booking to a cabin.
Well first day of snow cabin was freezing . Only complaint I would have is the cabin didnt have heater . But the heating  blanket made our sleep a good night sleep . Quite comfortable  place , very  neat and clean. Good for quick stop or even long stay. Cabin was much better than  some of the weird hotel rooms I've stayed in sydney.  So I'd definitely  recommend
We have great experience there especially their staff are friendly and available for you even in night time whenever you need their assistance.

Worst experience they didn’t know how to serve the customer after getting the property they behave very rude and didn’t return the bond So i didn’t recommend uh this place kindly don’t waste ur money and time

Honestly Rakesh was amazing, we only decided on the day that we would make the trip over the long weekend, Rakesh explained exactly what we needed to do and even reminded us about chains  for tyres, we stayed in the 3 bedroom and the kids loved it when we woke up to the snow fall then only a 20 min drive up to the mountains to see heavy snow fall. will deffs be going back here!

visited snow mountain for the first time and booked this place, just 18 min drive to ski centre, cozy and warm rooms, heater in bed, everything is there to keep us warm, best hospatility, its rare to see nowdays, i travel for work and stay at many places, but this really hit so good, clean and tidy rooms. any time avilable for service, thank you so much ☺️☺️☺️☺️

Rakesh a wonderful host, very helpful
Amenities block pristine and warm.
Everything you could need.

I loved my time at Kosciuszko tourist park! I stayed in the kosci off-season and it's close location to charlottes Pass made this the perfect base camp for the summit hike. The new managers are super helpful and made sure I was enjoying my stay. I will be back!
Very accomodating as we booked last minute thank you!!
Absolutely amazing..Clean and well maintained…. Loved the stay..

Great high altitude location, clean amenities. Enjoyed camping for few nights, plenty of national park walking tracks nearby. Night sky on a clear night is amazing for stargazing. Will camp again, definitely recommend.
Cozy little cabins,  clean toilets,  quiet,  peaceful and beautiful place. Watch out for the possums! They can get into your car and cabin looking for food!!

Amazing!! , service, location, cleanliness!!
We’ll be back!!

We stayed in one of the mountain chalets, beautiful views of the kangaroos and surrounding forest. Very quiet and clean. A short sunrise hike to the waterfall is a must. Staff were available and friendly.

Review for Kosciuszko Tourist Park

We had a great stay at Kosciuszko Tourist Park. The campsite was spacious, making it easy to set up and relax. Access to amenities was well-communicated via SMS, which was convenient. The toilets and showers were impressively clean, adding to the comfort of the stay. The only slight downside was the possums—they got a bit too interested when we started cooking! But overall, it was a pleasant experience, and we’d definitely consider staying again.

Dang. The Brazilian chick who runs this place keeps a tight ship. The bathroom is the cleanest I have ever seen. And the nicest smelling. A great place to bunk while climbing Australia's highest mountains.
Excellent cabin and convenient location close to the national park attractions.

No thrills camp ground. Very quiet and kangaroos grazing. Only stayed in summer months. No hand soap in mens toilets in 2 stays over 3 months. No gas/hot water/ stove tops in camp kitchen. Pot holes everywhere.
Bathrooms very good except for the 6 minute shower machine that constantly beeps.
BEWARE OF THE POSSUMS AFTER DARK, NO SHY AND WILL CLIMB OVER EVERYTHING AND LEAVE THEIR MARKS.
Lovely camping site for a good value.

Our extended family camped in 3 sites during the summer school holidays, and had a great time! Facilities were clean and well maintained, and we had no reason to bother management for anything. The large kitchen/dining area was most welcome during windy/rainy weather. We will come again.

Just had an amazing stay. Loved how close Sawpit Creek was to all the amazing activities the snowy mountains have to offer. waking up every morning to mobs of kangaroos and wallabies grazing so close to the tents was so lovely. So many walks close by and swimming in clear, beautiful creeks and rivers was just a treat. I would highly recommend staying. There's something for everyone to do of all ages.

Our group of 9 camped at Kosciuszko Tourist Park for 6 nights and loved it. The campsites were grassy and set among beautiful snowgums. The kangaroos grazed near our tents but did not disturb us. It was spacious and quiet. The amenities block is modern and clean with nice hot showers. We particularly loved the large, clean functional kitchen/dining building. The staff were friendly and helpful. We'll be back!

Stayed overnight to have a wander up the mountain, the place was fantastic, staff were very friendly and accommodating, amenities were modern and immaculate. I'd definitely stay again.

Thanks very much for the stay.

Davy

Great little spot just inside the national park (you will have to pay for the day passes)
Great amenities - bathrooms and toilets are in great conditions, recreation room and camp kitchen is slightly old but clean and tidy and basic cleaning equipment provided.
Good sized camping sites. A few good walking trails nearby.

Onsite manager is not very welcoming, hardly even spoke a few words, just handed over piece of paper and ignored me. it would be so much nicer with a friendly greeting or at least a smile. We stayed in cabin, which was Ok, but beds were very old and very uncomfortable, they desperately need updating or you will not sleep well at all which impacts the whole experience.
Had a great night stay camping at powered site. They have nice kitchen and good showers. All camp is surrounded with snow gums. Owner was kind to let me check in late on Christmas day.

Perfect place to start the journey to Kosciuszko summit and walking tracks

Clean safe area. Great cabin. Kids enjoyed it.
Great location.

Wonderful customer service.

We stayed for a night with our van before going to hike to the mount Kosciuszko.
It is perfectly located, well maintained and well equipped.
We enjoyed our stay, the fire in the evening and the kangaroos in the morning
In the heart of nature ! But the kitchen had no hotwater or the hotwater was turned off.
It was a really nice place to stay. Very clean, organised and high tech. Wonderful to see wildlife in the night

Great setting, friendly service, good price.
This is a great location if you are planning to spend time on the National Park.

Great place but don’t feed possums. Facilities are well maintained and clean (particularly bathrooms). Staff very helpful and friendly. We stayed in a cabin but have previously camped. The only drawback is the possums who are extremely demanding about being fed. Take the advice of the rangers and dont feed them. The aggressive ones will then leave you alone and you are more likely to be visited by mums with their bubs. Otherwise be prepared to be kept awake all night by them fighting outside or trying to break into your cabin or tent. If you do camp then the only way to keep your food safe is to keep it in your car.
Beautiful setting! So peaceful

Our family booked a long weekend at the Tourist Park and it was an ideal location to explore Kosciusko National Park, soak up the wild life and kick back in front of our own fire pit. Whilst the cabins are slightly older, they are clean, spacious and great value for Money! Our host were welcoming and ensured our stay was relaxing.
Make sure you take the time to walk through the park and say hellos to the Roo’s and Wallabies. Looking forward to booking a other weekend in the new year
Beautiful place to stay, the cabins are clean and functional
Booked the chalet for the weekend and it was fantastic. Facilities were great, Friendly service and convenient location Positioned well between perisher and jindabyne.
I had a great stay at Kosciusko Tourist park. The managers Keith and Shraddha are lovely and the location is great, very peaceful and walking tracks right on your doorstep.
A great place to stay. Also the amenities were spotless. Very friendly staff. Thankyou.  I was in a camper trailer, so didn't see the rooms.

The cabins are a little aged but that is OK.  The cabin we used was perfectly acceptable and met our needs.  I'd passed Sawpit Ck on many occasions in the past but  stopped and used the cabin for one night and camped for the other two.

The camping facilities were very good (also very clean and serviceable) set in a lovely picturesque location.

Finally, the young couple who managed the park were lovely.  Very courteous and accommodating and clearly very settled in the routine of the daily tasks.  They cared about doing a good job which is a revelation in Australia these days.

There is much to commend and nothing about which to complain.

Camping in nature, amongst the gums, close to Kosciuszko summit walk, near Thredbo river,
lots of wildlife, wallabies, kangaroo and deer, nature walks close by
Great camp kitchen, and toilet shower block is very nice also,
Beautiful area, nice accommodations, loved our stay.

We had such a wonderful stay at KosiPark Heritage Cabin.
We so loved the natural  beauty of your park and the great walks nearby and the comfort of No 10 cabin.
Your willing support and the helpful information  you gave us was so very appreciated.
I recommend this KosiPark to anyone who would enjoy a natural mountain experience.
John and Robbie

We camped for a week in October and had an amazing time. The weather was great, the service from staff was outstanding. They accommodated our every request. Facilities were new and well cared for - bathroom facilities especially were all new and very modern. We loved camping there and would highly recommend to anyone looking to explore the mountains!!

We have stayed a few times at Kosipark . So peaceful - perfect place to unwind.
Amenities were ultra clean . Nice hot water for a shower after a day of hiking was lovely.
Nice and close to Jindabyne and Thredbo .The cedar cabins were our pick - everything you needed. True ensuites weren’t attached but you ended up meeting people on the way.

We stayed for one week in a peaceful, comfortable cabin. It was very clean with a well equipped kitchen, good bathroom, plenty of space and comfortable beds. There was also good TV reception. We had great views out of the windows to the trees and the bush land at the edge of the spacious park. We made good use of our outdoor camp fire and enjoyed seeing kangaroos, deer and possums. Our host was very polite and helpful. We would definitely stay here again when visiting Kosciuszko National Park. There are great walks nearby.
Very nice facilities. We stayed a total of three nights and had a great time doing local hikes and cooking in the big kitchen building 10/10 would recommend
Great place to stay. Toilets are spotless.

I camped overnight on an unpowered site. I was pleasantly surprised by how modern and clean the communal showers/toilets were. The sites are sheltered from the wind and with firewood and fire starters being available for purchase, it made for a wonderful and relaxing night (made even better by the absence of traffic noise).
In terms of the natural environment, I had both kangaroo and deer grazing, in plain sight, about 20 metres from my tent, stunning views of the stars, and shaded by an abundance of trees.
I would thoroughly recommend staying for longer and using the park as a base camp for your cycling/hiking/driving day trips.

We recently stayed here as a family and what a great experience it was. The tourist park is in the national park nestled amongst the Snow Gums. We stayed in the upgraded chalets. The rooms were super, clean, comfortable, with heating to keep you warm. The bathroom was quite luxurious. There was a playground and basketball for the kids, a recreation room and kids had a great time riding their bikes around the grounds and loved sitting around the outdoor fire at night. With visits by the local fauna. The staff were also lovely and helpful.

It’s a great base for exploring the area with Perisher 10 min away, Thredbo 30 min, Jindabyne 10 min. Also some easy bush walks can be done from the tourist park. Great place to stay in both winter and summer. We will definitely be back!

Yesterday we stayed with our caravan. We booked online although I did call first to ensure I had the details correct. My call was promptly answered and the information clearly relayed.
We loved our stay, the amenities were great the site beautiful amongst the gum trees with well made fire pits. Visits from possums and wallabies were an highlight of this natural setting. Our welcome was warm and friendly- even with the Halloween greeting 😃. Thank you

After battling 70-80 kph winds at the Ngarigo campground, where we endured a tough night of tent camping, we were in desperate need of some shelter and comfort. We’d often driven past this park but never really considered staying. I'm so glad we finally gave it a chance.

The cabin we booked was a perfect retreat—nestled among the bushes and completely shielded from the relentless winds. It was exactly the peaceful haven we needed. The kitchen was well-stocked with everything we needed to whip up a hearty, hot meal, and the bathrooms were spotless, with consistently hot water—a blessing after the rough night we had.

We slept soundly and woke up feeling rejuvenated, thanks to the cozy cabin and the serene surroundings. The manager truly made our stay even more special, going above and beyond to ensure we were comfortable and had everything we needed.

This park offered us not just a place to sleep, but a much-needed respite from the elements, and I would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a peaceful, well-maintained, and welcoming place to stay.

I recently stayed at Kosciuszko Tourist Park, and I have to say, it was a fantastic experience. The accommodation was great—Very Affordable, clean, comfortable, and perfect for a peaceful getaway. The location is truly amazing, surrounded by nature and offering beautiful views, making it an ideal spot for relaxation or adventure in the mountains.

The service was excellent throughout my stay. The staff were incredibly helpful, always available when needed and willing to go the extra mile to ensure a pleasant experience. I highly recommend Kosciuszko Tourist Park for anyone looking for a serene escape with outstanding hospitality.

Horrible people to deal with and charge extra for a small child under 3 years of age,

When we booked it said childen under 3 are free. On top of all that they charge you $300 deposit and u have to call them to give it back to you.

Place is an absolute rip off for basically a freezing shed.

We stayed in the renovated cabin which was really well finished. The bathroom was surprisingly luxurious and even had heated floors. Management was always helpful. A great location for a snow holiday or in summer, spring and autumn to enjoy the National Park.

Trop chère pour le service! Et le personnel est désagréable
Je suis déçue, je n’y retournerais pas
Great cabins and camping spot. Beautiful location

We had a great stay at Kosciusko tourist park enjoying evening camp fires amongst the gums and only a short drive up to Perisher for snow activities during the day. The tourist park is lovely with an excellent camp kitchen, park for the kids and lots of native animals around to spot and enjoy. Thanks Keith for a great stay, see you next year!

Well make shore you do your homework and pick a site for your size van as you will end up in a under size site and no water connection if U ask to move they will charge you extra for changing site no tv reception no mobile reception so be prepared to site around fire and be invaded by possum plague as they will go through everything left outside
Excellent location and facilities! We found this place in 2023 and have been back every year since!
We’ve had a decent stay at cabin 13 over the weekend.
A nice easy place to stay in our van when at the snow fields. Clean facilities and a very convenient location. Staff are helpful.

Staying here for the night was great! The location was only 20 minutes from Perisher also 20 minutes from the ski tube! The facilities were great and made waking up and heading up the mountain so much easier! The showers and kitchen were kept clean for sharing with others. We had an awesome fire with a spacious area for our car to go for camping. Will definitely be staying here when we come back to the snowy mountains!
Cozy and warm chalet with a private fire pit.
That’s great place
It was a great Experienced. We recently stayed for 2 nights. Clean and very near to Perisher Valley. Highly recommended!
All good
Had an amazing time staying here, woke up to amazing snow on the last day, very convenient being close to the slopes and Jindabyne. Great service and facilities, amazing place.

We have stayed here several times in a 2 bedroom cabin. The cabins we stayed in were basic, but clean and comfortable. Fantastic location for skiing or other snow based activities with only a short drive to Perisher.

The staff were not helpful at any point. The actual park is quite nice and the showers were very good however booking our site was made very difficult by the owner. We emailed and told him over the phone what cars/vans were expected to come but on arrival he insisted we had too many cars despite our transparency throughout the booking process and that it was “impossible” for us to fit on our pre booked site. I insisted we give it a go and sure enough we all fit on one site. He made us book a second site prior to arrival which in hindsight we probably didn’t need. Even on arrival he mentioned possibly needing a third site booked. After we parked up on the one site he was content that we all fit however the process was not made easy and I was not impressed with his service.
The park is not very well maintained. The toilets are not cleaned every day as indicated and the staff are not pleasant. The price is expensive for what it is.
Absolutely beautiful nature lit camp ground. With impeccable amenities.

Great location, nice and warm cabins.
Friendly staff. Very clean toilets.

We had misunderstanding communication about the check-out conditions, however , the team of the park were very promptly to solve.

Look forward to staying again in a couple of weeks.

Me and my partner just came back from the long weekend there and the amenities were spotless, staff were very helpful and the quick wifi was definitely a bonus, will be back this season and many more seasons to come

Our family of 5 stayed in a cabin just after Easter and really enjoyed our week here.  The park was clean and well kept, and was very quiet due to being out of school holiday season.  There wasn't much spare space in the cabin and we could have done with a bit more storage for bags etc.  We were glad of the heating as the temperatures dropped (and snow fell).  Staff were friendly and helpful.  Access to the national park from here was very easy.  It was about 10 minutes back to Jindabyne for supplies and 35 minutes to Thredbo.  Autumn was quite picturesque and the weather was cold and unpredictable.  Wallabies, deer and possums were active in the park at night.  We'll be back to continue our adventures at Koscuiszko.
Je suis très déçue par cet endroit que ce soit le lieu ou le propriétaire du parc (c’était sale, il y avait un problème de chauffage, le wifi était très lent). Je n’y retournerais pas

I stayed at the Kosciuszko Tourist Park with my daughter in May 2024.

We camped in our swag in a powered site (so I could charge my phone and run an electric blanket).

They park had great amenities, including warm showers and large camp kitchen with BBQ and a good playground.

Each site has its own fire pit and wood can be purchased for $20 from reception.

The staff were very friendly and accomodating

The park is next to the sawpit creek picnic area; there were several short works close by aswell.

Close to Perisher and Charlottes Pass, even close to Thredbo. Plenty to see and do.

Watch out for those cheeky possums at night though, they are Hellene on getting your food!

Exceeded our expectations, especially in the off-ski season. We will be back!
We go to Kosciusko Tourist Park every Easter and it is always an awesome weekend. Service is great and always clean amenities. I can highly recommend to all those that love the outdoors.
We had a powered campsite, very spacious, the owners are very friendly and it was close to the ski fields.
Such a great spot!!!

the place is nice but it's not worth the detour, it's still expensive. On the other hand, the national park is really nice. You can find a better place down Jindabyne or even a free camp on the way to Charlotte Pass

My recent trip to Kosciuszko went anything but to plan. With they weather turning miserable, and being very underprepared for the weather it could have been a total disaster. Arriving on a Saturday evening in the pouring rain i was dreading having to setup camp. The friendly staff were kind enough to let me upgrade my campsite to a cabin on such short notice, taking off what i had already spent on the campsite and even finding me a bit of a deal on the cabin. This allowed to me get warm and dry which was a god send before the next day running in the hills. All in all the Campsite is well situated only being an easy 30 minuite drive from Charlottes Pass and 45 minutes from Thredbo. Great amenities, great location, great staff and even has (extra cost) really good high speed wifi. I definitely plan on visiting again.

Kosi Park should have a little refreshment, it's outdated and not worth the price. You have to pay for wifi, which is horrible! On the other hand, the national park is magnificent, and I recommend hiking in this area.
disappointed, expensive for what u get !

“I recently stayed at Tourist Park and had a fantastic experience! The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful throughout my entire stay. From check-in to check-out, they went above and beyond to ensure I had everything I needed. Their knowledge of the local area and willingness to assist with any questions or requests made my stay even more enjoyable. The park itself was well-maintained and offered a variety of amenities for guests to enjoy. Overall, I highly recommend Tourist Park for anyone looking for a great vacation spot with exceptional service from the staff.”

Nestled in the serene Snowy Mountains, Kosciuszko Tourist Park offers a delightful camping experience. My stay at camp was simply superb. The picturesque surroundings, well-maintained facilities, and friendly staff made for a memorable retreat. With a range of outdoor activities nearby and easy access to amenities, this park is a must-visit for nature lovers seeking a tranquil getaway. Highly recommended for a blissful escape amidst nature's embrace.

I've been staying at Sawpit's Kosciuszko Tourist Park for years at Easter, and it never, ever disappoints. A great bush setting, comfortable lodging and close to both Perisher, Guthega and Charlotte's Pass as well as Jindabyne. Plenty of wildlife around, as you're right in the middle of the scrub. You won't find a better setting for a bush holiday with all the comforts of home.
Not the first visit and not last at Kosciuszko TP , always great.

Our fave caravan park! We camp here every year in a large group and love the place.  Budget friendly prices with 5 star amenities and friendly staff who make sure that we enjoy our stay.  So lucky to be able to camp in this beautiful part of the National Park

This is a caravan and camping park with cabins. It is set in the National Park and so boasts an incredible natural setting.  The amenities are excellent and I would certainly return for another visit even though you have to pay an extra $17 per day to enter the National Park. We had a memorable weekend.
Stayed here with the family over Easter in a cabin. A great spot to stay, kitchen was well equipped and the outdoor fire area was amazing. Will definitely be going back.

Wonderful quiet bush setting, many of the caravan sites come with fire pits.  Conveniently located in Kosciuszko National Park.
Bathroom facilities are new and very clean. Pleasant helpful staff.  Would definitely stay there again.

The possums ruled the park like a gang. We could not leave anything out side the caravan 1 of the possums kept chasing my wife to the point she is afraid of them .apart from that we did enjoy the park in the day time just not at night

Great spot very quiet. The toilets are like brand new. Right in the national park. About 15 min from Jindabyne. A bit on the pricey side. 🤷‍♂️ but I guess it’s in a national park.

Couple of hiking spots in walking distance.

G’day Team

As you may know we have stayed at the park few times and needless to say, it has been a very peaceful holiday.Nothing more to ask and nothing more to say.it’s only gotten best every year and look forward for many more visits.

10/10.

Regards
Ninad

We just camped here. Toilets are very clean. We are camped inside Kos National Park and hence vehicle pass required. Adds $17 per day to cost. Compensated by the nature surrounding and access to walking tracks. Yes, horse flies are plenty. Various insects visit us regularly. Possums check out camping set up during night times. So, don’t leave any eatable or bins out. Despite all these we enjoyed our camping there just for the atmosphere. It’s not crowded. The stay is memorable. Service in general is top notch. They work hard to maintain the reputation

Winter is probably better as you're less likley to care so much about the cabins age & quality. Very old dirty carpet, fridge iced up, kettle & toaster need replacing. Table needs stripping back as varnish has become very sticky & dirty. The cabin was not value for money given time of year, its old age & internal  state. Queen bed instead of a lounge area was a suprise. Very much setup for max capacity for winter travellers.
The amenities block is fantastic being refurbished. Perfect for vanners & campers.  Friends stayed in a van so we checked these things & had a bbq there. Toielts block newly refurbed & fantastic. Music sittong around the fire pit was great every night.
Possum population is huge so put everything away & cover stuff or you'll have poo & wee on everything inc car. Really its a natual location that was mostly very enjouable.
We had a great stay and enjoyed the numerous walking trails around the park.

Loved our stay at Kosi tourist park! We stayed in a cabin that was fully equipped with cooking utensils. Clean and wonderful amenities. Location was peaceful, in the mountains with plenty of kangaroos hopping around. There were a few local trails on site too which we enjoyed. Sushil, the manager was very kind and accommodating to us. Would definitely recommend :)
We stayed in our camper van for two nights. Such a gorgeous woodland site. The facilities were brand new and spotlessly clean.  The friendly owner made us feel especially welcome. We hope to return.

I had to share my fantastic experience from my recent stay at this amazing facility. The beds were really comfy, and the kitchen was fully equipped. The cabin was excellent. The clothes rack and heaters were also quite useful to us since they helped dry our outerwear. We were welcomed with extreme warmth and friendliness by the reception personnel. The nicest thing, though, was the location—Perisher was just a fifteen-minute drive away! Anyone searching for a fantastic spot to stay in the region should definitely check out this facility, in my opinion. Once again, I want to thank you for everything

I'm very disappointed by this place, even though it has potential! The rooms don't smell good. The sheets hadn't been changed in a long time, they smelled bad and there were stains. The common areas are not cleaned every day... for the price of the cabins, I think it's a steal.

The guy who runs the place is a total drop kick, rude, obnoxious and simply not qualified both professionally and personally to be in a customer facing role. Go anywhere else on the planet but here.

** not sure who you are replying to but has nothing to do with me or my experience. I suspect this is a 'standard' response you use for negative feedback, as you couldn't possibly respond to each one accurately due to the volume of negative feedback you must receive on a regular basis. But to accurately respond to your inaccurate response, I will outline: by reflecting on my experience, I cannot see any situation that you would not respond with insults, aggression and profanity. And the very fact you have got so confused over which complaint is what, only goes to reinforce my initial recommendation to anyone wondering whether to stay at this park or not remains; I recommend you consider going anywhere else in the world besides here.
New common bathrooms/showers are great. Staff is very helpful.

My husband and I stayed in the 2 bedroom cedar cabin, for three days. On arrival the room was lovely and warm. The bed has an electric blanket that keeps you nice and warm.
The cabin has everything you need, spare blankets, pillows and the kitchen is well equipped all you need to bring is your food.
The shower was lovely after a big day on the snow. You can have your own fire outside your cabin, the lovely staff will hook you up with wood, fire starters and even a lighter and there is even some where to sit.
One of the best things is it’s only a 15-20 minute drive to the snow, wow love that!
We had a beautiful view from our room what a great way to wake up every morning.
The park is lovely and clean and has everything from a camp kitchen, Landry, pool table, lounge area and a big tv for everyone to enjoy. This cabin has an undercover carport so even if it’s raining or snowing you can stay dry while packing or unpacking your car. I think it’s just the little things ❤️
Highly recommend this place for a getaway ,so peaceful.
We will definitely be coming back. 😘

A nice park within the National Park. 15mins or less to Perisher. Cabins are very basic, warm and comfortable. Amenities are clean, nice hot showers. Camp kitchen, also simple, but adequate. Probably the most affordable way to stay near the snow (but it's still not cheap).

Fantastic campsite, the site staff do an incredible job in the up keep of the grounds, all amenities were in pristine condition, plenty of friendly people around too making it the perfect site to stay for a camp trip in Kosciuszko Park.
Great place to stay. We camped. Noisy neighbours.

Very convenient position for the mountains situated inside the national park. Extremely quite, well spaced with clean modern facilities. Purchasing internet access is worth it as phone signal is very intermittent.

The owners were great help and extremely nice at accommodating. highly recommend for anyone looking for a cheap and convenient camp ground when coming down to the snow! had a great experience will definitely be back again

Services	      ========4.5/5
Facilities	========4.5/5
Cleanliness	========4/5
Comfort	========4/5
Value for money	========4.5/5
Location	========4.5/5
Free WiFi	========4.5/5

Quite convenient location, 12 min drive to Jindabyne and appro. 20 mins to Perisher main parking in the morning.
It's a tourist park, some facilities are shared for some accommodation types, shared bathroom (they have two in total, but only one of them is heated), shared kitchen and lounge room.
They also have a laundry room, $5.00 for wash and $5.00 for drying, and drying room in winter(cost unknown).

In terms of the room, we stayed in 6 people cabin, which has double bed, 1 bunk bed and 2 singles. linen will not provided EXCEPT for double, you either need to bring your own or hire at $15.00 each. Room was nice, clean and comfortable in general. Does what it says, but can not expect more(same across nearly all accommodations in Jindy during winter time tough). no mobile reception in the park, but you can buy internet at extra cost, $10.00 for 10GB if I remember correctly, it uses Starlink I believe.

You can see some kangaroos and possums and kids love them.

What a customer service as can be seen from owners response, caravan was parked on national park grounds 450 meters from caravan park , owner has used f words on so many occasions that i had to ask him to move away to keep a distance , and i have called police not him , he is now charged for fraud  for charging my credit card, bank and police is involved in it ,please share dash cam photos and videos, please read some other reviews, he has a history of intimidating and harassing guests and customers, he has mental issues, I would not recommend anyone to stay at that place , and report to police any issues if happens

Ps hav can you fit 6 ppl
In small car , he is making up stories
Stayed here to go skiing, only 15 minutes from Perisher. Cedar Cabins were clean and kitted out (no oven though). Quiet location, easy to get to. Kids loved local wildlife.

Such a beautiful Park to stay in.
Facilities are great and the service was amazing.
Can't wait to stay here again.

We stayed in one of the newly renovated 2 bedroom heritage chalets last weekend and absolutely cannot fault it. The room was exceptionally clean with the heater and electric fireplace running on arrival, the underfloor heating in the bathroom made the stay all the more comfortable in the cold winter climate and the wooden features throughout gave the cabin a fantastic feel. We would absolutely stay here again and recommend it to anyone thinking about booking!

Absolutely excellent camping sites, Im a below the knee amputee and I have no problems taking a shower I have a chair that goes with me in the shower and there's plenty of room to move freely whilst in the shower  toilets are always cleaned and fresh, the grounds are kept tidy, awesome layout an plenty of room, disability friendly which is just superb, very impressed!! the owner is a top bloke always up for questions and even ask him for some walking trails or tracks if you want to explore!
Lots of wildlife to keep you occupied while hanging around the fire. Hands down a really great environment to kick back, drying room and common room with kitchen.. everything you need right here not to mention perisher is 15km up the hill.

Definitely recommend!!!
Wonderful service, so understanding and willing to help. Beautiful location and super affordable, can't wait to go again :)

We love this place! The amenities are great and the local wildlife are plentiful and friendly.  The staff Here go out of their way to help with anything you need. The owner,Suchil is a great bloke with a can do attitude and nothing's a bother to him.. keep up the great work..

We had a mix up with our booking, we booked for 4 but we’re only provided bedding for two. They were happy to clear this up free of charge when we arrived. Cosy cabins amongst the bush highly recommend.
